known_gaps: External guideline artifacts are not cached or license-verified.
Clinical and safety review is incomplete.
Simulation set is below release coverage requirements.

exposure.sick_contact.wording: Have you been in close contact with anyone who has been ill?

observation.body_temperature.wording: Have you measured your temperature, and what was the highest reading?

patient.immunocompromised.wording: Do you have a condition or treatment that weakens your immune system?

symptom.abdominal_pain.wording: Do you have abdominal or stomach pain?

symptom.chills.wording: Have you had chills or shaking?

symptom.confusion.wording: Have you or someone with you noticed new confusion, disorientation, or unusual drowsiness?

symptom.cough.wording: Do you also have a cough?

symptom.diarrhea.wording: Have you had diarrhea or unusually loose stools?

symptom.duration.wording: How long have you had the fever or felt feverish?

symptom.dyspnea.wording: Are you having any difficulty breathing?

symptom.headache.wording: Do you have a new or severe headache?

symptom.neck_stiffness.wording: Is your neck unusually stiff or painful to move?

symptom.non_blanching_rash.wording: Do you have a new purple, rapidly spreading, or non-blanching rash that does not fade when pressed?

symptom.severe_pain.wording: Are you having extreme pain or discomfort anywhere?

symptom.skin_infection_features.wording: Do you have a new painful red or swollen skin area, wound, or drainage?

symptom.sore_throat.wording: Do you have a sore throat?

symptom.systemic_unwellness.wording: Do you feel severely or unusually unwell overall?

symptom.urinary_symptoms.wording: Do you have pain or burning when urinating, urgency, or needing to urinate more often?

guideline_behavior.rationale: Interview behavior derived from guidelines may change with recommendations.

local_jurisdiction.rationale: Local pathways and administrative rules may change quickly.

safety_critical.rationale: Safety behavior and escalation knowledge require frequent monitoring.

stable_semantic.rationale: Atomic patient-reportable concepts are relatively stable but still require periodic review.

terminology_mapping.rationale: Terminology and interoperability baselines have independent releases.

clinical_guideline.rationale: Daily automated monitoring provides early detection for high-impact guideline changes; full review occurs only when change is detected or the review date is reached.

nice_guidance.rationale: NICE guidance is versioned and may be amended; weekly monitoring detects changes without implying weekly clinical re-review.

public_health_guidance.rationale: Public-health pages can change between formal releases; weekly monitoring is appropriate for the research package.

terminology_server.rationale: Terminology servers publish versioned content; monthly baseline monitoring balances stability and acquisition cost.

assertions: Possible infection requires assessment for source, vulnerability factors, and abnormalities of behaviour, circulation, or respiration.
Sepsis may present non-specifically and without high temperature.
Impaired immune function and recent procedures increase vulnerability.
artifacts.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over

assertions: Fever, headache, neck stiffness, and altered cognition form a red-flag combination.
A rapidly progressive or non-blanching petechial or purpuric rash is a warning feature.
Absence of the full combination does not rule out serious illness.
artifacts.title: Bacterial meningitis and meningococcal disease

assertions: Sepsis warning features may include confusion, fever or chills, extreme discomfort, and breathing difficulty.
Potential sources include respiratory, gastrointestinal, urinary, and skin infections.
artifacts.title: About Sepsis
